summary: A small two-story rectangular modern building with a flat roof and horizontal window bands. The walls use a pale yellow textured block (sandstone-like) between smooth white corner/trim bands, a brick ring near the roof, and stone slabs capping the roof. The building sits on a raised white slab border surrounding a dirt excavation area.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with slightly raised rim/base and flat roof
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the central vertical plane (front/back identical), consistent window alignment on both floors

elements: horizontal window bands on both floors (continuous narrow rows of glass panes)
vertical smooth white corner trim running full building height
brick band (one block tall) below stone slab roof cap
raised white slab border at ground level around footprint
flat recessed roof area filled with bricks surrounded by stone slab coping
rebuild_hints: Construct a rectangular footprint roughly 11 x 7 blocks, two full stories tall (~6 blocks high to roof cap).
Use smooth white blocks (quartz) for full-height corner columns and a continuous horizontal belt between floors; fill middle wall faces with sandstone textured blocks.
Place narrow horizontal windows: two rows (one per floor) of 1-block-tall glass panes spanning the central length with small vertical separations.
Add a one-block-tall brick band around the top edge, and cap with stone slabs forming a rim; inset the roof interior with brick blocks matching the top-down images.
Surround the building base with a one-block-wide white slab border sitting on ground level and leave surrounding area as exposed dirt.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone and quartz are visually matched but could be sandstone types (smooth, chiseled) or other light blocks; confidence provided per material.
Precise footprint dimensions: estimated from visible block counts in images; may be off by a few blocks.
Interior layout and presence of doors or internal partitions are not visible and therefore unknown.
Window pane type (glass pane vs glass block) assumed panes due to narrow horizontal windows but could be full blocks.
